description_long: This object is a compact, cylindrical bomb with a cluster of six round chambers arranged in a circular pattern, resembling the cylinder of a revolver. The chambers are tightly grouped and encased in a dark, metallic shell, giving the device a robust and industrial appearance. A single, elongated handle extends downward from the center of the cluster, suggesting a hand-held or deployable design. The surface is smooth and glossy, reflecting light in a way that highlights its hard, metallic construction. There are no visible buttons, wires, or timer displays on the exterior, and the object appears seamless and solid. The overall design is minimalistic, with a focus on functionality and durability.
description: A dark, metallic bomb with six cylindrical chambers arranged in a cluster and a single handle extending downward. The surface is smooth, glossy, and industrial in appearance.
description_view_0: Front view showing the six round chambers in a tight circular cluster with the handle extending straight down.
description_view_1: Slightly rotated view revealing the curvature of the chambers and the handle's attachment to the cluster.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, with the handle visible and the chambers forming a symmetrical pattern.
description_view_3: Another angled view, highlighting the uniformity of the chamber arrangement and the glossy finish.
attribute_detonation_mechanism: No visible detonation mechanism is present; the device may be triggered internally or via the handle.
